explanation: A superlative built through denial: "nothing / no one is as … as X" (富士山ほど美しい山はない). ほど names X's level and the final negative denies that anything else reaches it — the opposite move from plain ほど, which affirms a degree. As AはBほど〜ない it makes a simple comparison: "A is not as … as B". In casual speech くらい can replace ほど here.
formation: N ほど + 〜 + はない / A は B ほど + 〜ない

examples.jp: <ruby>母<rt>はは</rt></ruby>の<ruby>料理<rt>りょうり</rt></ruby>ほどおいしいものはない。
examples.en: Nothing tastes as good as my mother's cooking.

examples.jp: <ruby>今年<rt>ことし</rt></ruby>の<ruby>夏<rt>なつ</rt></ruby>ほど<ruby>暑<rt>あつ</rt></ruby>い<ruby>夏<rt>なつ</rt></ruby>はなかった。
examples.en: No summer has ever been as hot as this one.

examples.jp: <ruby>富士山<rt>ふじさん</rt></ruby>ほど<ruby>美<rt>うつく</rt></ruby>しい<ruby>山<rt>やま</rt></ruby>はないと<ruby>思<rt>おも</rt></ruby>う。
examples.en: I don't think any mountain is as beautiful as Mt. Fuji.

examples.jp: <ruby>東京<rt>とうきょう</rt></ruby>の<ruby>冬<rt>ふゆ</rt></ruby>は、<ruby>北海道<rt>ほっかいどう</rt></ruby>の<ruby>冬<rt>ふゆ</rt></ruby>ほど<ruby>寒<rt>さむ</rt></ruby>くないです。
examples.en: Winters in Tokyo aren't as cold as winters in Hokkaido.
